thought.text:
Lots of nested ifs with constant conditions `i > 1`, `i > 2`, ... where `i = 200` is set once and never changes. If we propagate constant `i = 200`, all conditions fold to true. Then we get a straight-line program. This needs: const propagation across stores (not just loads in the same BB), or recognizing that `%i` is initialized once and never overwritten so it's effectively const 200.

In task3, `i = 200; while (j<n) { if (i > k) { ... }}` will after mem2reg become a PHI for `i` if it ever changes. But here `i` never changes, so it could just be value 200 throughout the loop. Let me see:
